After synthesizing the discussion, directly address and fulfill the user's original request instead of stopping at a discussion summary.

The P2P discussion is complete. Use the discussion file as context, then directly fulfill the user's original request in this session.
Discussion file: {{discussionFile}}
The original user request is: "{{request}}"
Do not stop at another discussion summary.

p2p.settings_rounds_hint: Number of discussion rounds (more = deeper analysis)
p2p.settings_hop_timeout_hint: How long to wait for each agent to respond. Increase for complex tasks.
p2p.settings_extra_prompt_hint: Additional instructions for all participants (e.g. "Reply in Chinese")

single_agent_prompt.title: Add more agents?
single_agent_prompt.tip_multi: Using multiple agents from different types/providers helps break fixed thinking patterns and usually gives the best results.
single_agent_prompt.tip_rounds: For P2P discussions, 2 rounds usually work best.
single_agent_prompt.dont_show_again: Don't remind me again for this account
single_agent_prompt.tip_history: Multi-agent discussions share files and keep fuller history. One-to-one discussions are treated as one-off discussions.

openspec.title: OpenSpec
openspec.audit_implementation_prompt: Perform a strict implementation audit for {{reference}} against its OpenSpec artifacts. Identify every mismatch, omission, regression risk, edge-case gap, and missing test; then fix the code, tests, and any required supporting changes. If the change artifacts need to move with the implementation, update the relevant OpenSpec files under {{reference}} in the same task. Do not stop at a report. Deliver the implementation in full spec compliance.
openspec.audit_spec_prompt: Perform a strict specification audit for {{reference}}. Strengthen scope, requirements, acceptance criteria, edge cases, failure modes, dependencies, and testability; remove ambiguity and internal inconsistency; then directly update the change artifacts under {{reference}} (proposal, design, specs, tasks) until they are implementation-ready. Do not stop at review notes.
openspec.implement_prompt: Drive the implementation of {{reference}} aggressively. Break the work into concrete sub-tasks, dispatch sub-agents with clear ownership, integrate their output, resolve gaps against the spec, add or update tests, and verify the finished result. Keep the OpenSpec artifacts under {{reference}} aligned with the implementation as you go instead of leaving follow-up notes. You own orchestration, technical decisions, and final acceptance.
openspec.achieve_prompt: Take {{reference}} to done using the full OpenSpec workflow. Inspect all remaining tasks and artifacts, complete the required implementation and spec work, directly update proposal/design/specs/tasks under {{reference}} where needed, close outstanding gaps, sync affected specs if needed, and archive the change once it meets completion criteria. Do not stop at status reporting.
openspec.propose_from_discussion_prompt: Generate an OpenSpec change from the recent discussion. Extract the goal, scope, key requirements, and acceptance criteria, list unclear points as follow-ups, and write the actual change artifacts under openspec/changes/ (proposal, design, specs, tasks) instead of stopping at a draft note.
openspec.propose_from_description_prompt: Generate an OpenSpec change from the description below. Organize the goal, scope, key requirements, and acceptance criteria, list missing details as follow-ups, and write the actual change artifacts under openspec/changes/ (proposal, design, specs, tasks) instead of stopping at a draft note.

localWebPreview.title: Local Web Preview
localWebPreview.sandboxNote: The preview runs in a sandboxed iframe. Some apps may work better in a new tab.
localWebPreview.empty: Enter a localhost port and path, then open the preview.
localWebPreview.initialPathNote: Path sets only the initial page. After load, navigation is scoped by port, not by this path.

cron.title: Scheduled Tasks
cron.name: Task Name
cron.advanced_help: Format: minute hour day month weekday — e.g., 0 9 * * 1-5 = weekdays at 9am
cron.shell_hint: Tip: Shell sessions can run scripts directly — just enter the script path (e.g. /path/to/script.sh)
cron.read_only_scope: This cron job belongs to another server or tab and is temporarily read-only here.

fileBrowser.unsavedChanges: You have unsaved changes. Discard them?
fileBrowser.fileTooLarge: File too large to save (> 1 MB)
fileBrowser.saveTimeout: Save timed out — please reload file

prompt.title: Enable the new-user guide?
prompt.body_1: Your first main session is ready. If you are new, this short guide will walk you through the core controls.
prompt.body_2: It starts with the basic buttons, then ends with how multi-agent discussions work best.

new_main.title: Create a main session here
new_main.body_1: Use this button to create a new main session for a project.
new_main.body_2: Main sessions are the entry point for terminal, chat, repo, and discussion workflows.

new_sub.title: Create a sub-session here
new_sub.body_1: Use this button to add a worker or helper sub-session under the current project.
new_sub.body_2: Sub-sessions are useful for side tasks, reviews, experiments, and parallel work.

discussions.title: Open discussion history here
discussions.body_1: This opens your discussion history so you can review previous discussion runs and results.

repo.title: Open the repository panel here
repo.body_1: Use this button to inspect repository status, branches, commits, PRs, and CI.

cron.title: Open scheduled tasks here
cron.body_1: Use this button to create and manage scheduled tasks for the current project.

view_toggle.title: Switch between chat and terminal here
view_toggle.body_1: Use this toggle to switch between chat view and live terminal view for the current session.

p2p_defaults.title: Configure default discussion participants here
p2p_defaults.body_1: Open this menu and choose Config when you want to set which sessions join @all by default.
p2p_defaults.body_2: That is also where you quickly choose default discussion rounds.

p2p_send.title: Start discussions from the input box
p2p_send.body_1: Type @@all(discuss), @@all(review), or another mode to start a shared discussion quickly.
p2p_send.body_2: Agent discussions work best with 2 or more agents from different providers or types. That helps break fixed thinking patterns and usually gives better results.
p2p_send.body_3: Multi-agent discussions share files and preserve fuller history. One-to-one discussions are treated more like one-off discussions. In practice, 2 rounds are a strong default.

management.title: Shared Context
management.projectRelationshipLine1: Personal memory works without any enterprise.
management.projectRelationshipLine2: Shared project context starts only after you enroll a repository into the currently selected enterprise.
management.projectRelationshipLine3: Workspace is optional and always belongs to that same enterprise. It is a grouping layer, not a separate owner.
management.projectRelationshipLine4: Scope controls how far a project shares context inside the enterprise. It does not change which enterprise owns it.
management.belongsToWorkspaceValue: Optional subgroup inside the selected enterprise.
management.sharesWithProjectValue: Only this enrolled repository shares context.
management.sharesWithWorkspaceValue: Projects in the same workspace share context together.
management.sharesWithEnterpriseValue: All enrolled projects in the current enterprise can share context.
management.chooseSharingLevelProject: One repository, one shared context boundary. Use this for most teams.
management.chooseSharingLevelWorkspace: Several related repositories inside one workspace share context together.
management.chooseSharingLevelEnterprise: The broadest scope. Use only when all enrolled projects should share the same context pool.
management.scopeProjectDescription: Share context only for this enrolled repository.
management.scopeWorkspaceDescription: Share across projects grouped under the same workspace.
management.scopeEnterpriseDescription: Share at the enterprise level across all enrolled projects.
management.scopeSourceWithoutWorkspace: No workspace selected. This project still belongs to {{enterprise}}.
management.policyExplainLine1: These settings control how this shared project behaves when a provider cannot fully support shared context or when the remote shared state is unavailable.
management.policyExplainLine2: Use the stricter options for team-owned codebases. Use the more permissive options only when you need continuity over strict consistency.
management.allowDegradedHelp: Allow providers that can only inject shared context in a reduced or message-side form. Turn this off if the team requires consistent behavior across providers.
management.allowLocalFallbackHelp: Allow this machine to keep using its local processed context when shared remote context is stale or temporarily unavailable.
management.requireFullSupportHelp: Only allow providers that fully support normalized shared-context injection. This is the strictest setting.
management.requireFullProviderSupportHelp: Only allow providers that fully support normalized shared-context injection. This is the strictest setting.
management.processingSummaryLine1: Raw activity is staged locally, then materialized into processed local context on delayed jobs.
management.processingSummaryLine2: Shared projects can replicate processed context to the remote shared store after local materialization succeeds.
management.processingSummaryLine3: This panel manages enterprise policy and authored knowledge. It does not directly run the materializer.
management.processingCloudSyncNote: This setting is saved in the cloud for the selected server and synced into the daemon when it is connected or restarts.
management.processingServerRequired: Select a server before managing processing settings.
management.processingProviderNote: Choose both the SDK/backend and the model. The daemon will use this pair when it runs local shared-context materialization jobs.
management.processingBackendNote: The backend decides which runtime executes the model. The model alone is not enough.
management.processingUiGap: This setting is server-scoped, not enterprise-global.
management.processingOperationalLine1: If no server-saved value exists, the primary model defaults to "sonnet".
management.processingOperationalLine2: The backup model is optional and defaults to unset.
management.processingOperationalLine3: Different servers can still use different saved processing models. This panel edits the selected server only.
management.memorySummaryLine1: Local personal memory keeps both pending raw events and processed summaries so you can see what is still waiting and what has already been compressed.
management.memorySummaryLine2: When personal cloud sync is enabled, only processed summaries replicate to your user-scoped cloud memory pool for cross-device continuity.
management.memorySummaryLine3: Shared memory stats show enterprise-scoped processed context that other enrolled projects can reuse.
management.personalSyncDescription: Choose whether processed personal memory should replicate to your account-wide cloud memory pool.
management.personalSyncToggle: Enable cloud sync for personal memory
management.personalSyncHelp: Turn this on to share processed personal memory across your devices. Leave it off to keep processed personal memory local-only.
management.memoryQueryDescription: Search local personal memory, cloud personal memory, and enterprise shared memory using the same filters.
management.memoryProcessedNote: Processed memory shows compressed projections only. Raw pending events stay local; cloud personal memory and enterprise shared memory store processed summaries, not raw chat transcripts.
management.memoryPendingDescription: These events have not been materialized yet. They remain local raw activity and are never uploaded as raw cloud memory.
management.memoryProcessedDescription: These entries are compressed processed memory. Local views show processed summaries beside local pending events; cloud and enterprise views contain processed summaries only.
